title: Probability For a New One-Hit-Wonders Game
body:
Gisella fits a logistic regression model to the data she collected. Gisella includes
an intercept in her model and does not use regularization. Gisella uses the number of
opponent fans as the first feature in her model $$x_1$$ and the number of One-Hot Wonders fans
as the second feature $$x_2$$. The bias column is the first column of the the design matrix X. She
assigns each dance battle a label of $$y_i=1$$ if the One-Hot Wonders won, and $$y_i=0$$ if they
lost. The fitted model parameters are shown.
##figure1.gif
